channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lebanon shelter supports displaced LGBT community amid conflict
item.description: Mohammed, a gay man from southern Lebanon, fled his hometown after Israeli airstrikes damaged his home near Sidon in March 2026. Like many displaced people in Lebanon, he faced limited options, but as...

item.title: Charlene Ruto to Participate in Zimbabwe’s Nyuki Marathon to Promote Beekeeping
item.description: Charlene Chelagat Ruto, daughter of President William Ruto, is set to participate in the upcoming Nyuki Marathon in Harare, Zimbabwe, running from May 14 to May 20. Through her Smachs Foundation, Char...

item.title: Tamil Nadu Election Results Create Political Uncertainty as TVK Falls Short of Majority
item.description: Following the Tamil Nadu assembly elections, the political landscape in the state is highly uncertain. The Vijay-led Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) emerged as the single-largest party with 108 seats i...

item.title: Bauchi Governor Bala Mohammed Joins APM To Contest Bauchi South Senate Seat
item.description: Bauchi State Governor, Bala Mohammed don declare say him go run for Bauchi South Senatorial District seat under the Allied Peoples Movement (APM) ahead of 2027 general elections. Naija News report say...

item.title: Family in Embu Appeals for Help to Find Son Missing in Qatar Since 2023
item.description: A family in Embu county is desperately searching for Harrison Waiganjo, who has been missing since he travelled to Qatar in 2023 looking for better job opportunities. Soon after arriving in the Gulf c...

item.title: Communications Minister Anika Wells repays $10,116 after breaching parliamentary travel rules
item.description: Communications and Sports Minister Anika Wells has reimbursed over $10,000 to taxpayers following an Independent Parliamentary Expenses Authority (IPEA) review that found she breached parliamentary tr...

item.title: Elderly woman seriously injured in Brunswick after alleged hit-and-run
item.description: A 71-year-old woman is in a serious condition in hospital after being allegedly struck by a car in Brunswick, Melbourne. The incident occurred around 4:15 pm on Wednesday when the woman was crossing S...

item.title: Trump inspects Reflecting Pool renovation, compares it to previous project under Obama
item.description: President Donald Trump visited the National Mall on Thursday to inspect the ongoing renovation of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. During the visit, he drove his motorcade across the drained pool...

item.title: Edmonton Council Approves Rezoning for New Tower Project Near University of Alberta
item.description: Edmonton City Council has approved a rezoning application that will allow a new tower project to proceed near the University of Alberta campus. The decision comes after several discussions and consult...

item.title: Mustafa Hassanali introduces new standards and support for Miss World Tanzania
item.description: For over 20 years, Mustafa Hassanali has been a key player in Tanzania's fashion scene, known for his style, creativity, and influence. Recently, he stepped into the beauty pageant world as the nation...

item.title: Thomas Detry Affirms Commitment to LIV Golf Despite Saudi Funding Withdrawal
item.description: LIV Golf faces uncertainty after the Saudi Arabia-backed Public Investment Fund announced it would cease funding following the 2026 season. The breakaway tour, known for its high purses and team-based...

item.title: Australia’s Democracy: Resilience and Emerging Challenges
item.description: Australia has long been regarded as a bastion of stable democracy, resisting the rise of populist right-wing movements that have challenged political norms elsewhere, notably in the United States and ...

item.title: A gentle and light-hearted murder mystery led by clever talking sheep
item.description: Kyle Balda’s film The Sheep Detectives offers a charming and easy-going take on the murder mystery genre, built around an unusual yet endearing premise: a group of sheep who secretly understand human ...

item.title: Three men killed in gang-related drive-by shooting in Bonteheuwel
item.description: On Wednesday evening, three men were killed in a drive-by shooting in Bonteheuwel, Cape Town, in what authorities believe was a gang-related attack. The incident took place at the corner of Kiaat Aven...

item.title: Pilot whales in the Strait of Gibraltar raise their calls to communicate over ship noise
item.description: In the Strait of Gibraltar, a critically endangered sub-population of long-finned pilot whales is facing challenges due to human-made noise. Despite their common name, pilot whales are actually large ...

item.title: Thunder Take 2-0 Lead Over Lakers with Strong Performances from Gilgeous-Alexander and Holmgren
item.description: In a commanding display on Thursday night, the Oklahoma City Thunder defeated the Los Angeles Lakers 125-107, taking a 2-0 lead in the Western Conference semi-final series. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and...

item.title: West Bengal Madhyamik 2026 Results Announced: 86.83% Students Pass
item.description: The West Bengal Board of Secondary Education has officially declared the Madhyamik examination results for the year 2026. According to reports, a total of 86.83% of students have successfully cleared ...

item.title: Angine de Poitrine: Quebecois duo’s rise with microtonal rock and iconic masks
item.description: Angine de Poitrine, a Quebec-based duo, has captivated audiences with their inventive microtonal rock and elaborate papier-mâché masks. Formed by Khn and Klek, childhood friends from Saguenay, the pai...

item.title: Bongani Fassie Reflects on Sobriety and Musical Evolution Ahead of New EP
item.description: Bongani Fassie, son of the late South African music legend Brenda Fassie, has opened up about his personal journey of growth, sobriety, and resilience as he prepares to release his new EP, 'Skomline'....

item.title: Post-election violence escalates in Kolkata following BJP’s electoral victory
item.description: Following the Bharatiya Janata Party’s sweeping victory in the West Bengal elections, Kolkata has witnessed a surge in political violence, causing fear among residents. On Wednesday night, two men sho...
